title: Glucose 10%
content_markdown:
##### Type

Hypertonic glucose solution – containing 0.1g dextrose per ml

##### Presentations

10% glucose – in 250ml in collapsible plastic flask

##### Actions

Glucose is the main energy source for the body cells, especially the brain. 
 Onset: 30 – 60 seconds. 

#### Uses

Treat hypoglycaemia (BGL <4mmol/L, and patient unable to eat/drink)

#### Adverse Effects

- Tissue necrosis if allowed to escape from the vein

- Hyperglycaemia / hyperosmolality

#### Contraindications

Known hypersensitivity

#### Pregnancy and Breastfeeding

##### Pregnancy

- Safe to use

##### Breastfeeding

- Safe to use

#### Doses

##### Adult and Paediatric

- Up to 2.5ml/kg (0.25g/kg) slowly over 5 minutes 
- Continue to check patency of vein
- May repeat dose once, if LOC or BGL has not improved.

#### Special Notes

- Patients who respond to glucose 10% should eat, to prevent later development of further hypoglycaemia.
- Recheck BGL following treatment.
